scenes.summary: In ancient Egypt, Hebrew baby boys face a deadly decree from Pharaoh. Moses' mother hides him in a basket on the Nile to save him, watched from afar by his sister Miriam.
beats: Beat 1: Jochebed placing baby Moses in a basket among the reeds of the Nile.
Beat 2: Miriam watching from a distance as Pharaoh’s daughter discovers the basket.
Beat 3: Pharaoh’s daughter deciding to keep baby Moses and calling for a Hebrew nurse.

scenes.summary: Moses grows up in Pharaoh’s palace but feels his Hebrew identity. After witnessing an injustice against his people, he flees Egypt and starts a new life as a shepherd in Midian, marrying Zipporah.
beats: Beat 1: Young Moses in fine clothes at Pharaoh’s palace, looking thoughtful and conflicted.
Beat 2: Moses intervening as an Egyptian beats a Hebrew slave, confronting the injustice.
Beat 3: Moses living quietly as a shepherd in Midian with his wife and family.

scenes.summary: Moses encounters the burning bush on Mount Horeb; God calls him to lead the Israelites out of Egypt and reassures him with His name and promises of support.
beats: Beat 1: Moses noticing the unburnt, flaming bush and approaching it cautiously.
Beat 2: God speaking to Moses from the bush, instructing him to remove his sandals and announcing His identity.
Beat 3: Moses listening in fear and hesitation as God commands him to go to Pharaoh, promising aid with his brother Aaron.

scenes.summary: Moses returns to Egypt and demands Pharaoh to free the Israelites. Pharaoh refuses, leading to ten devastating plagues before finally releasing the people.
beats: Beat 1: Moses and Aaron standing before Pharaoh demanding freedom for their people.
Beat 2: Pharaoh refusing with scorn, refusing to acknowledge the Lord.
Beat 3: Scenes hinting at the plagues and Pharaoh’s eventual ordering the Israelites to leave.

scenes.summary: The Israelites hastily flee Egypt with their belongings after centuries of slavery. Pharaoh regrets releasing them and pursues them with his chariots.
beats: Beat 1: Israelites carrying bundles and children, leaving Egypt rapidly.
Beat 2: Pharaoh ordering his officers to prepare chariots and chase after them.
Beat 3: Chariots racing in the distance with dust clouds behind them.

scenes.summary: The Israelites arrive at the edge of the Red Sea, trapped between the water and Pharaoh’s approaching army. Fear and panic grip the people as families prepare for the worst.
beats: Beat 1: Families gathered at the sea’s edge, children clutching parents, animals and carts packed nearby.
Beat 2: Pharaoh’s chariots visible in the distance, dust rising.
Beat 3: Moses observing the fearful crowd and praying for guidance.

scenes.summary: God commands Moses to lift his staff over the sea, parting the waters and creating a dry path with towering walls of water on each side, inspiring the people to move forward in faith.
beats: Beat 1: Moses raising his staff over the sea, a wind starting to roar.
Beat 2: Water pulling back and splitting, revealing a path of dry earth.
Beat 3: People gasping and beginning to walk cautiously into the sea passage.

scenes.summary: The Israelites cross through the sea pathway with a mix of fear and hope. Children reach toward the shimmering walls of water, and families support each other as they make their way forward.
beats: Beat 1: People stepping carefully on the seabed; sand squishing under sandals.
Beat 2: A father holding his daughter’s hand as she looks nervously at the water.
Beat 3: Children pointing to fish in the walls of water while others pray quietly.

scenes.summary: At the back, Moses and strong men guide the slower travelers. A pillar of cloud shields the Israelites from Pharaoh’s army following closely behind in chariots.
beats: Beat 1: Moses and men helping elderly and weary travelers.
Beat 2: A glowing pillar of cloud standing between the Israelites and the approaching Egyptian army.
Beat 3: Chariots pounding the seabed road, horses neighing and soldiers urging forward.

scenes.summary: Pharaoh’s chariots enter the path but get stuck as wheels break and horses panic. Soldiers realize God is fighting against them and begin to retreat in fear.
beats: Beat 1: Chariot wheels sinking into sticky earth and twisting.
Beat 2: Horses rearing and screaming as soldiers struggle.
Beat 3: Soldiers shouting warnings to retreat as the ground traps them.

scenes.summary: Moses stretches out his hand again and the wind ceases. The towering walls of water crash back down, engulfing the Egyptian soldiers, chariots, and horses. The sea returns to its natural state.
beats: Beat 1: Moses raising his staff solemnly as wind stops suddenly.
Beat 2: Water walls collapsing with a roaring crash over the chariots.
Beat 3: The sea calm and deep again, sunlight beginning to rise.

scenes.summary: The Israelites celebrate their miraculous escape with song and dance. Miriam leads praise to the Lord. Moses speaks to Eli about trusting God when afraid, encouraging obedience and faith as they prepare to continue their journey into freedom.
beats: Beat 1: Miriam playing tambourine and leading joyful singing and dancing.
Beat 2: Eli laughing and running to join the festive crowd.
Beat 3: Moses calming Eli and encouraging trust and obedience while gazing across the sea toward their new future.

characters.name: Moses
characters.description: Leader chosen by God to deliver Israelites from slavery and guide them to freedom.

characters.name: Miriam
characters.description: Moses’ sister, a comforting and brave presence encouraging the people.

characters.name: Eli
characters.description: A little boy among the Israelites, represents the innocent and hopeful future.

characters.name: Eli’s Mother
characters.description: Protective mother, comforting and comforting Eli during the escape.

characters.name: Pharaoh
characters.description: The Egyptian king pursuing the Israelites, stubborn and commanding.

characters.name: Jochebed
characters.description: Moses’ mother who saved him as a baby by placing him in the Nile basket.

characters.name: Pharaoh’s Daughter
characters.description: Egyptian princess who discovers baby Moses and arranges his care.

characters.name: Jethro
characters.description: Moses’ father-in-law who welcomes him and whose daughter Moses marries.

characters.name: Zipporah
characters.description: Moses’ wife, daughter of Jethro, supportive figure in his life.

characters.name: Aaron
characters.description: Moses’ brother who helps him speak to Pharaoh as commanded by God.

characters.name: A Young Girl
characters.description: A girl near the front who questions how to cross the sea.

characters.name: A Father
characters.description: Father holding his scared daughter’s hand crossing the sea path.

characters.name: An Old Woman
characters.description: Elderly Israelite fearful about dying in the wilderness.

characters.name: A Man
characters.description: Israelite shouting about the seeming hopelessness of their situation.

characters.name: Pharaoh’s Captain
characters.description: Officer hesitant about entering the sea path, fearful of God’s power.

subpanels.description: Jochebed gently placing baby Moses in a woven basket nestled among tall Nile reeds.

Dialogue context: Jochebed: Stay safe, my son..
